Article.ArticleTitle: Intracoronary Imaging for Percutaneous Coronary Intervention Guidance.

Abstract.AbstractText: Intracoronary imaging (ICI) is an important adjunct to invasive coronary angiography for the assessment of lesion morphology and guidance of percutaneous coronary intervention. Extensive data, including several randomized clinical trials and meta-analyses, support the use of ICI to improve clinical outcomes. Despite a modest increase in utilization, adoption remains limited, even in the setting of a robust evidence base and guideline recommendations. In this review, we provide a comprehensive overview of contemporary ICI modalities, including clinical indications, comparative advantages and limitations, and key supportive evidence. We also offer practical guidance on their use, procedural considerations, and address common pitfalls, as well as address the identification and clinical implications of high-risk "vulnerable" plaque features.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declaration of competing interest Dr. Brilakis has received consulting/speaker honoraria from Abbott Vascular, American Heart Association (associate editor Circulation), Boston Scientific, Cardiovascular Innovations Foundation (Board of Directors), Cordis, Elsevier, GE Healthcare, IMDS, Medtronic, Recor Medical, SIS Medical, Teleflex, and Terumo; research support: Boston Scientific, GE Healthcare; owner, Hippocrates LLC; shareholder: Cleerly Health, LifeLens Technologies, Inc, MHI Ventures, Stallion Medical, TrueVue Inc. Dr. Sandoval reports consulting and/or speaker honoraria from Abbott Vascular, Boston Scientific, CathWorks, Cleerly, GE Healthcare, HeartFlow, IMDS, Medtronic, Philips, Roche Diagnostics, and Siemens Healthineers; research support from Cleerly, HeartFlow, and Shockwave Medical; owner, Systole LLC; and holds patent 20210401347 with others. Dr. Arnold H. Seto reports honorarium from SIS Medical. Dr. Evan Shlofmitz is a consultant for Abbott Vascular, ACIST Medical, Boston Scientific, Gentuity, HeartFlow, Medtronic, Philips, Radiaction, Shockwave Medical, and Terumo; serves on the Speaker’s Bureau for Amgen and Novo Nordisk; and has Equity with 4C Medical, Vantis, and Xenter.
